Name: BM25
Description: Robust bag-of-words retrieval function that ranks documents based on the appearance of query terms. Serves as a highly effective standard baseline for sparse retrieval tasks in search engines.
Key Technique: Utilizes probabilistic term-weighting over TF-IDF to score document relevance. Introduces term frequency saturation and document-length normalization to prevent long documents from dominating results.
Architecture: N/A
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Designed to improve upon basic TF-IDF by addressing its flaws, specifically by capping the impact of repeated words. Works exceptionally well for exact keyword matching and remains a lightweight fallback for modern neural systems.

Name: Transformer
Description: Foundational sequence-to-sequence model that completely eliminated the need for recurrence and convolutions. Relies exclusively on attention mechanisms to draw global dependencies between input and output tokens.
Key Technique: Multi-head self-attention allows every position in a sequence to attend to every other position in O(nÂ²) time. Uses positional encodings to inject sequence order information without relying on sequential processing.
Architecture: Stacked encoder and decoder architecture. Each layer contains multi-head self-attention and a position-wise fully connected feed-forward network, augmented with residual connections and layer normalization.
(Pre)-training Objective: Supervised sequence-to-sequence learning on the WMT 2014 English-German dataset. Utilized standard cross-entropy loss to optimize machine translation accuracy.
Rationale: Developed to overcome the sequential bottleneck of RNNs, enabling massive parallelization during training. This parallel nature, combined with direct global context, made it the universal backbone for modern AI scaling.

Name: InfoNCE
Description: Powerful contrastive loss function used extensively in representation learning. Trains models to distinguish a matching positive pair from a large pool of negative pairs within a given batch.
Key Technique: Formulates the contrastive learning problem as a categorical cross-entropy classification task over a batch of samples. Pulls positive pairs closer in embedding space while simultaneously pushing away multiple negative examples.
Architecture: N/A
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Provides a highly efficient way to learn robust, generalized representations without requiring explicit manual labels. Maximizes the amount of learning signal extracted from a single forward pass by using in-batch negatives.

Name: MaxSim
Description: Fine-grained similarity scoring mechanism pioneered in Late Interaction retrieval models. Computes the relevance between a query and a document by comparing their multi-vector representations at the token level.
Key Technique: Computes the dot product between every query token and every document token instead of pooling a sequence into a single vector. Selects the maximum document token score for each query token and sums these maximums to produce the final relevance score.
Architecture: N/A
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Introduced to solve the information bottleneck inherent in single-vector models like DPR. Preserves token-level details, enabling highly accurate fuzzy keyword matching while remaining computationally cheaper than full cross-encoders.

Name: Cross-Entropy
Description: Standard classification loss function used across deep learning. Measures the performance of a classification model whose output is a probability value between 0 and 1.
Key Technique: Quantifies the difference between two probability distributions: the true distribution (labels) and the predicted distribution (model outputs). Heavily penalizes predictions that are both confident and wrong.
Architecture: N/A
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Provides a smooth, differentiable gradient that directs the model to increase the predicted probability of the correct class. Works universally well by naturally aligning with maximum likelihood estimation principles.

Name: Sigmoid Contrastive Loss
Description: Scalable alternative to the standard softmax-based contrastive loss used in massive vision-language models. Operates solely on image-text pairs and avoids global normalization across the batch.
Key Technique: Computes pairwise sigmoid loss independently for every item, treating the alignment task as a series of binary classification problems. Eliminates the need to gather representations across different AI accelerators.
Architecture: N/A
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Standard softmax loss requires cross-device synchronization, limiting batch sizes and scaling. Isolating the loss calculation to independent pairs unlocks theoretically infinite batch sizes, leading to better performance at scale.

Name: BERT
Description: Landmark natural language processing model that introduced deep bidirectional pre-training. Learns rich contextual representations by looking at both the left and right context of a token simultaneously.
Key Technique: Introduced the Masked Language Modeling (MLM) objective, randomly masking 15% of the input tokens and forcing the model to predict them. Bypassed the unidirectional constraint of previous language models.
Architecture: Multi-layer bidirectional Transformer encoder. Utilizes WordPiece tokenization and incorporates special [CLS] and [SEP] tokens to handle classification and sentence-pair tasks.
(Pre)-training Objective: Joint objectives of Masked Language Modeling (MLM) and Next Sentence Prediction (NSP) on BookCorpus and English Wikipedia.
Rationale: Unidirectional models could not capture the full context of a word in a sentence. Bidirectional approach allowed for highly robust contextual embeddings, revolutionizing fine-tuning performance on downstream NLP tasks.

Name: ViT
Description: Paradigm-shifting model applying a standard Transformer directly to images. Proved that convolutions are not strictly necessary for achieving state-of-the-art computer vision performance.
Key Technique: Treats an image as a sequence of discrete visual tokens by splitting it into fixed-size patches. Linearly projects these patches into flat vectors fed into the transformer just like text words.
Architecture: Standard Transformer Encoder. Relies on learnable positional embeddings to retain spatial information and appends a [CLS] token to aggregate global image features for classification.
(Pre)-training Objective: Supervised image classification on massive proprietary and public datasets like JFT-300M and ImageNet-21k.
Rationale: Leverages the massive scalability and efficiency of transformers for computer vision. Removing CNN inductive biases allows the learning of more generalized patterns when provided with sufficient data scale.

Name: T5
Description: Unified framework casting every NLP problem into a text-to-text format. Allows the exact same model, objective, and hyperparameters to be used across highly diverse tasks.
Key Technique: Relies on task-specific prefixes (e.g., 'translate English to German:') to condition output natively. Introduced a novel span-corruption pre-training objective that masks consecutive tokens rather than isolated words.
Architecture: Transformer encoder-decoder architecture utilizing relative positional embeddings instead of absolute ones, allowing better generalization to varying sequence lengths.
(Pre)-training Objective: Span-corruption objective on the massive Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus (C4). Learns to reconstruct missing text spans autoregressively.
Rationale: Previous models required task-specific architectures or classification heads for different downstream tasks. Streamlined the entire NLP pipeline, demonstrating that a single generative framework could achieve SOTA across the board.

Name: DiT (Document Image Transformer)
Description: Specialized vision transformer explicitly adapted for Document AI. Captures complex visual layouts and structures of document images without requiring OCR text as an input.
Key Technique: Applies generative masked image modeling specifically to the document domain. Forces the reconstruction of visual pixels of masked document patches, implicitly learning text boundaries and layouts.
Architecture: Standard ViT (Transformer Encoder) architecture initialized and trained specifically for high-resolution document processing rather than natural scenes.
(Pre)-training Objective: Masked Image Modeling (MIM) on the IIT-CDIP Test Collection, a massive dataset of unannotated document images.
Rationale: General vision models trained on natural scenes perform poorly on text-dense documents. Created to build a domain-specific visual backbone that understands document semantics natively.

Name: BEiT
Description: Self-supervised vision model treating image patches like discrete text tokens. Introduced a BERT-like pre-training approach for the visual domain, bridging the gap between NLP and CV pre-training methodologies.
Key Technique: Predicts discrete visual tokens for masked image patches instead of reconstructing raw pixels. Tokens are generated beforehand by a pre-trained discrete variational autoencoder (dVAE).
Architecture: Standard Transformer encoder taking corrupted image patches as input and outputting representations that map to the discrete visual vocabulary space of the dVAE.
(Pre)-training Objective: Masked Image Modeling (MIM) predicting the discrete token IDs of masked patches based on unmasked visual context.
Rationale: Predicting continuous raw pixels is overly complex and focuses on high-frequency noise. Predicting discrete tokens forces the learning of meaningful visual concepts similar to linguistic words.

Name: BART
Description: Denoising autoencoder designed for pre-training sequence-to-sequence models. Combines the bidirectional encoding capabilities of BERT with the autoregressive decoding strengths of GPT.
Key Technique: Introduces a highly flexible pre-training scheme where text is corrupted with arbitrary noising functions, such as sentence permutation or text infilling. Learns to reconstruct the original clean text.
Architecture: Standard Transformer encoder-decoder architecture. The encoder processes corrupted text bidirectionally, while the decoder generates original text autoregressively with causal masking.
(Pre)-training Objective: Sequence-to-sequence reconstruction loss on a massive text corpus by applying multiple noise functions to the input.
Rationale: Previous models were either exclusively encoders or exclusively decoders. Bridged this gap to create a model excelling at generative comprehension tasks like abstractive summarization.

Name: GPT
Description: Foundational causal language model series. Demonstrated that unsupervised learning produces highly capable generalist language models by predicting the next word on massive datasets.
Key Technique: Relies entirely on autoregressive generation, using masked self-attention to ensure a token can only attend to previous tokens in the sequence, preserving strict left-to-right generation.
Architecture: Transformer decoder-only architecture. Strips away the encoder block entirely, stacking multiple decoder blocks with causal masking.
(Pre)-training Objective: Standard unsupervised next-token prediction on diverse internet text corpora. Maximizes the likelihood of predicting the correct subsequent token based on prior context.
Rationale: Chosen because human language generation is inherently sequential. Scaling up this simple objective proved models could absorb vast amounts of world knowledge without explicit fine-tuning.

Name: ColBERT
Description: Dense retrieval model gracefully resolving the tradeoff between efficiency and effectiveness in search. Introduces a late interaction mechanism to calculate fine-grained similarity without feeding query-document pairs jointly through a heavy transformer.
Key Technique: Represents queries and documents as matrices of token-level embeddings rather than single pooled vectors. Computes similarity using the MaxSim operator to find the maximum cosine similarity between each query token and document tokens.
Architecture: Dual BERT encoder architecture (one for queries, one for documents). Token-level embeddings are stored offline for documents and matched efficiently at inference time via MaxSim.
(Pre)-training Objective: Pairwise contrastive ranking loss ensuring the positive document scores higher than sampled negative documents for a given query.
Rationale: Full cross-encoders are too slow for searching millions of documents, while single-vector bi-encoders lose crucial token-level details. Late interaction preserves cross-encoder accuracy while operating at bi-encoder speed.

Name: CLIP
Description: Foundational multimodal model learning highly robust, zero-shot transferable visual concepts. Bridges the gap between vision and language by training on hundreds of millions of internet-scraped image-caption pairs.
Key Technique: Utilizes massive batch sizes and InfoNCE contrastive loss to align images and text in a joint embedding space. Maximizes cosine similarity of correct image-text pairs while minimizing similarity of incorrect pairs.
Architecture: Dual Encoder architecture comprising a Vision Encoder (ViT or ResNet) and a Text Encoder (Transformer). Pooled outputs of both towers are mapped via linear projections to a shared multimodal space.
(Pre)-training Objective: Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (InfoNCE) on a massive proprietary dataset of 400 million image-text pairs to predict which text snippet belongs to which image.
Rationale: Traditional vision models relied on narrow, human-annotated datasets limiting generalization. Using natural language supervision from the web allows the learning of an incredibly broad and flexible visual vocabulary.

Name: Pix2Struct
Description: Visually-situated language model explicitly designed to understand visually rich contexts like webpages and documents. Bypasses OCR entirely by parsing screenshots directly into underlying structured representations.
Key Technique: Introduces a novel variable-resolution input strategy dynamically adapting the patch grid to handle varying aspect ratios without distortion. Learns to map visual elements directly to HTML or structured text.
Architecture: Asymmetrical ViT Encoder and Transformer Decoder architecture. The vision encoder processes raw screenshots, and the autoregressive decoder generates structured text output.
(Pre)-training Objective: Screenshot parsing objective learning to reconstruct masked HTML structures directly from a given webpage screenshot.
Rationale: Standard document AI relied heavily on fragile OCR pipelines failing on complex web layouts. Proved that an end-to-end vision-to-text generative approach natively captures complex layout structures.

Name: SigLIP-SO400m
Description: Advanced vision-language model vastly improving the CLIP architecture by changing the fundamental loss function. The shape-optimized 400M parameter version serves as the foundational vision backbone for many state-of-the-art MLLMs.
Key Technique: s
Architecture: Retains the Dual Encoder architecture of CLIP (ViT for vision, Transformer for text), heavily optimized for scaling batch sizes across distributed hardware clusters.
(Pre)-training Objective: Sigmoid Contrastive Learning on massive web datasets, pushing matching pairs to probability 1 and non-matching pairs to probability 0 independently.
Rationale: Scaling batch sizes in CLIP requires immense cross-device communication that crashes hardware. Sigmoid loss unlocks theoretically infinite batch scaling, leading to significantly better visual-semantic alignment.

Name: DPR
Description: Seminal model popularizing dense retrieval for Open-Domain Question Answering. Conclusively demonstrated that retrieving passages using dense neural embeddings significantly outperforms sparse algorithms like BM25.
Key Technique: Uses dual encoders to represent queries and passages as dense 768-dimensional vectors. Calculates relevance instantaneously using the inner product (dot product) between these two vectors.
Architecture: Two separate, independent BERT encoders: a Query Encoder and a Passage Encoder. The [CLS] token representation serves as the global dense vector for the sequence.
(Pre)-training Objective: Contrastive learning with in-batch negatives and carefully selected hard negatives (passages retrieved by BM25 missing the answer) forcing fine-grained semantic learning.
Rationale: Sparse retrievers fail on lexical mismatch. Solves this by projecting text into a semantic space where meaning, rather than exact vocabulary, determines closeness.

Name: LayoutLMv3
Description: State-of-the-art Document AI model jointly modeling text, layout, and image features within a single unified transformer. Eliminated the need for complex, separate CNN visual backbones used in previous iterations.
Key Technique: Unifies masking objectives across modalities, predicting masked text words, masked 2D bounding box coordinates, and masked image patches simultaneously. Forces deep cross-modal alignment at the token level.
Architecture: Multi-modal Transformer encoder taking 1D text tokens, 2D spatial coordinate embeddings representing layout, and linear projections of raw image patches.
(Pre)-training Objective: Masked Language Modeling (MLM), Masked Image Modeling (MIM), and Word-Patch Alignment (WPA) to learn fine-grained spatial mappings across the document.
Rationale: Documents are inherently multimodal, relying on the interplay of text and layout. Unified architecture captures these interactions natively, setting benchmarks for visually rich document understanding.

Name: LLaMA 3
Description: Highly optimized, open-weights large language model series. Achieves state-of-the-art performance through massive data scale and extreme architectural efficiency improvements over previous generations.
Key Technique: Utilizes Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) to drastically reduce KV cache memory for long contexts, and Rotary Positional Embeddings (RoPE) to model sequence order. Expands vocabulary to 128k tokens for better multilingual efficiency.
Architecture: Decoder-only Transformer employing SwiGLU activations instead of standard ReLU and using RMSNorm for both pre- and post-normalization to ensure stable training at immense scales.
(Pre)-training Objective: Standard autoregressive next-token prediction on over 15 trillion tokens, followed by post-training alignment using Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Direct Preference Optimization (DPO).
Rationale: Designed to push scaling law boundaries while maintaining open accessibility. Architectural tweaks specifically target inference efficiency, allowing massive models to run on standard hardware with massive context windows.

Name: LLaVA
Description: Pioneering multimodal model bridging pre-trained vision encoders and large language models. Enables standard text-based LLMs to see and seamlessly follow visual instructions.
Key Technique: Connects a frozen visual encoder to a frozen LLM via a simple trainable projection layer. Leverages high-quality multimodal instruction data generated by language-only GPT-4 to train reasoning about images.
Architecture: Pre-trained CLIP ViT visual encoder and a pre-trained LLM decoder connected via a 2-layer MLP projection layer mapping visual patches directly into the LLM's text embedding space.
(Pre)-training Objective: Stage 1 freezes models and trains projector for feature alignment. Stage 2 freezes vision encoder and trains projector and LLM on complex visual instruction data.
Rationale: Training massive multimodal models from scratch is prohibitively expensive. Proved highly capable VLMs can be rapidly built by stitching together existing SOTA uni-modal models with a lightweight adapter.

Name: RAG
Description: Seminal framework combining a neural retriever with a generative LLM. Allows the model to ground generative outputs in external, non-parametric knowledge bases, significantly reducing hallucinations.
Key Technique: Marginalizes generation over retrieved documents, treating documents as latent variables. Concatenates the query and retrieved texts in the generator's context window to synthesize factually grounded answers.
Architecture: Bi-Encoder Retriever (Dense Passage Retriever) and an Encoder-Decoder Generator (BART-large) connected via retrieved text tokens passed into the generator's context.
(Pre)-training Objective: End-to-end training using marginal log-likelihood. Generator and Query Encoder are fine-tuned jointly to produce the correct answer, while the Document Index (FAISS) is typically frozen.
Rationale: LLMs cannot update internal parametric memory easily and frequently hallucinate rare facts. Explicitly separates factual knowledge storage from reasoning capabilities to solve this.

Name: InternViT-6B
Description: Massive 6 billion parameter Vision Transformer designed to capture extremely high-resolution and robust visual features. Bridges the parameter gap between vision towers and massive language models in modern MLLMs.
Key Technique: Aggressively scales standard ViT architecture utilizing dynamic resolution tiling strategies to process massive image sizes without downsampling. Incorporates advanced layer scaling and RMSNorm to stabilize 6B parameter training.
Architecture: Scaled-up Transformer Encoder explicitly designed to act as the massive visual sensory organ for the InternVL family of multimodal models, outputting high-dimensional patch embeddings.
(Pre)-training Objective: Progressive combination of Contrastive Learning (aligning with text) and generative Masked Image Modeling to ensure deep representation learning and semantic alignment.
Rationale: As LLMs grew to 70B+ parameters, standard 300M CLIP vision encoders became a bottleneck. Scaling the vision encoder directly solved the modality capacity gap to feed complex visual details to the LLM.

Name: Qwen2-VL
Description: Highly capable multimodal LLM setting the standard for Document AI and visual reasoning. Natively supports dynamic resolution, allowing processing of documents and UI screens without losing aspect ratios or fine details.
Key Technique: Introduces Naive Dynamic Resolution and 2D-Rotary Positional Embeddings (M-ROPE) to map any image of any aspect ratio into a dynamic number of visual tokens, perfectly preserving original visual structure.
Architecture: Integrates a dynamic-resolution Vision Encoder with a Qwen2 LLM decoder using multi-layer perceptron projectors to map variable numbers of visual tokens into the language space.
(Pre)-training Objective: Multimodal Next Token Prediction trained extensively on a massive mix of interleaved image-text data, video-text data, and specifically curated visually rich document datasets.
Rationale: Forcing a rectangular document into a fixed square destroys text readability. Dynamic resolution and 2D positional tracking allow inherent understanding of document layouts better than strict fixed-resolution models.

Name: InternVL2
Description: Advanced open-source Multimodal Foundation Model series pairing massive vision encoders with state-of-the-art LLMs. Achieves closed-source level performance on incredibly demanding high-resolution document understanding tasks.
Key Technique: Utilizes dynamic high-resolution tiling strategy to split large images into multiple 448x448 local tiles plus a global contextual tile. Employs progressive scaling to transfer vision weights from small LLMs to larger ones to save compute.
Architecture: Links the InternViT-6B visual encoder with various LLM decoders via an MLP projector. Concatenates tokens from the global view and all local tiles into a massive sequence for the LLM.
(Pre)-training Objective: Progressive training paradigm starting with vision-language alignment, followed by multimodal pre-training on interleaved datasets, concluding with Supervised Fine-Tuning on tool-use and reasoning tasks.
Rationale: Capturing global context and microscopic detail requires massive input resolution. The tiling strategy captures local detail, while the global view maintains overall semantic coherence.

Name: PaliGemma
Description: Lightweight Vision-Language Model optimized for fast and accurate multimodal tasks. Uses an unmasked, full-attention processing approach for images and text prompts, leading to highly robust performance on dense visual tasks.
Key Technique: Leverages SigLIP vision encoder and Prefix-LM masking to allow visual tokens and text prompt to attend to each other bidirectionally before generating the causal answer autoregressively.
Architecture: Fuses a SigLIP-So400m vision encoder directly with a Gemma-2B decoder model via a linear projection layer. Image and text tokens are concatenated without complex pooling, retaining spatial grids.
(Pre)-training Objective: Joint multimodal pre-training on a massive mixture of tasks (captioning, VQA, detection, OCR) at low resolution, followed by resolution upscaling stages before task-specific fine-tuning.
Rationale: Combining a best-in-class vision encoder and highly efficient language model creates a powerful, easily fine-tunable base. The full-attention prefix allows the LLM to deeply contextualize the image before answering.

Name: DocFormerv2
Description: State-of-the-art model for visually rich documents introducing an asymmetric pre-training approach. Vastly improves both efficiency and performance of document parsing over previous LayoutLM-style models.
Key Technique: Utilizes an asymmetric autoencoder design where a heavy encoder extracts joint multimodal features and a lightweight decoder reconstructs them. The decoder is discarded after pre-training, leaving a highly efficient encoder.
Architecture: Asymmetric Transformer Encoder-Decoder. The deep encoder processes multimodal inputs (text, vision, layout), while the shallow decoder is strictly used during pre-training for reconstruction tasks.
(Pre)-training Objective: Asymmetric Masked Modeling reconstructing both masked text tokens and continuous visual patches from the document image simultaneously.
Rationale: Standard symmetric pre-training is incredibly computationally expensive for high-resolution documents. A shallow decoder for reconstruction speeds up training significantly while forcing the encoder to learn deeper representations.

Name: Gemma 2
Dependencies: Transformer; Gemma; Gemini; GQA; RoPE; RMSNorm
Relationship: Uses; Extends; Distilled from; Uses; Uses; Uses
Description: High-performance series of open-weights LLMs designed to punch far above their weight class. Achieves this by using massive teacher models for knowledge distillation and an advanced interleaved attention architecture.
Key Technique: Alternates between local sliding window attention and global attention to balance context capture with memory efficiency. Employs Logit Soft-Capping to prevent logit drift and stabilize training for extremely deep networks.
Architecture: Decoder-only Transformer featuring Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) and RMSNorm pre/post normalization. Interleaved attention processes up to 8192 tokens efficiently by limiting full attention to every other layer.
(Pre)-training Objective: Autoregressive Next-Token Prediction combined heavily with Knowledge Distillation. Smaller models minimize negative log-likelihood against soft probability distributions generated by larger Gemini teacher models.
Rationale: Training small models from scratch on hard labels often limits reasoning depth. Distillation transfers world knowledge and reasoning nuance of massive models, allowing small models to rival competitors twice their size.

Name: ColPali
Description: Groundbreaking retrieval model designed specifically to solve the 'PDF bottleneck' in RAG pipelines. Completely bypasses error-prone OCR by applying late interaction directly to visual patch embeddings of document pages.
Key Technique: Adapts the ColBERT MaxSim operator for the visual domain. Matches text query tokens directly against a dense grid of raw image patch embeddings extracted from a document page to find visual regions answering the query.
Architecture: PaliGemma vision encoder combined with a ColBERT-style late interaction mechanism. Image patches map directly to a 128-dimensional token-level embedding space without generating intermediate text.
(Pre)-training Objective: Contrastive learning using InfoNCE loss combined with Late Interaction matching. Maximizes the MaxSim score between a user query and the image of the specific document page containing the answer.
Rationale: OCR pipelines destroy spatial context, fail on complex tables, and miss visual cues. Retrieving directly on visual patches preserves layout semantics and achieves state-of-the-art multi-page document retrieval.

Name: CoT
Description: Paradigm shift in how LLMs are queried, significantly boosting reasoning capabilities. Forces the model to articulate intermediate logical steps before outputting the final answer.
Key Technique: Prompts the model to generate a reasoning chain (latent variables) instead of standard input-output prompting. Decomposes complex problems into smaller, manageable conditional probabilities.
Architecture: N/A
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Standard prompting forces complex math or logic into a single forward pass token. Spreading computational FLOPs across multiple tokens vastly reduces errors on multi-step reasoning tasks.

Name: Vicuna
Description: Influential open-source chatbot created by fine-tuning LLaMA models on user-shared conversations. Proved high-quality conversational data could rapidly align base models to approach ChatGPT-level interaction quality.
Key Technique: Pioneered the use of crowdsourced, high-quality dialogue data (scraped from ShareGPT) for highly effective supervised fine-tuning. Focused on multi-turn conversation flow rather than single-turn instruction following.
Architecture: Strictly identical to the base LLaMA model (Decoder-only Transformer). Differentiation lies entirely in post-training alignment data and learning rate optimizations.
(Pre)-training Objective: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) using standard cross-entropy loss on approximately 70K high-quality, multi-turn user-shared conversations to learn human-like dialogue patterns.
Rationale: Base LLMs are autocomplete engines and do not naturally converse. Demonstrated that a relatively small amount of highly diverse, high-quality dialogue data unlocks powerful conversational agents.

Name: MinerU
Description: Highly precise, specialized parsing tool designed to extract structured markdown from complex PDFs. Crucial for generating high-quality RAG corpora from visually rich documents containing complex tables and mathematical formulas.
Key Technique: Replaces heuristic-based PDF parsing with a pipeline of specialized vision models. Utilizes layout detection models to segment pages and vision-to-text models to translate complex formulas and tables into precise LaTeX/Markdown.
Architecture: Sequential pipeline architecture rather than a single model. Orchestrates object detection models, OCR engines, and specialized VLM decoders to construct the final document representation.
(Pre)-training Objective: Various supervised tasks depending on the pipeline component, such as bounding box regression for layout detection and sequence-to-sequence reconstruction for formula parsing.
Rationale: Traditional PDF parsers jumble text reading order and destroy table structures, ruining RAG pipelines. Treating document parsing as a rigorous computer vision task preserves exact semantic structures required by LLMs.

Name: Adapter (Houlsby 2019)
Description: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique designed to adapt massive models to new tasks without updating all weights. Allows multiple downstream tasks to be trained efficiently while avoiding catastrophic forgetting.
Key Technique: Inserts small, trainable bottleneck feed-forward networks (Adapters) between fixed layers of a pre-trained transformer. Modifies less than 3% of total parameters during fine-tuning while original weights remain frozen.
Architecture: Small bottleneck Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs) injected sequentially after attention and feed-forward modules of a standard Transformer block.
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Full fine-tuning is computationally prohibitive and creates separate massive model copies for every task. Achieves near-identical performance while drastically reducing memory requirements and allowing inference hot-swapping.

Name: MLP Projector
Description: Simple but critical network component used in modern multimodal LLMs to align different modalities. Translates feature representations extracted from a visual encoder into the mathematical embedding space of the language model.
Key Technique: Acts as a bridge taking high-dimensional Vision Transformer output and mapping it linearly (or non-linearly) into the exact dimensionality required by the LLM's input layer.
Architecture: Typically a 2-layer Multi-Layer Perceptron. Consists of a linear layer, non-linear activation function (like GELU), and another linear layer to match the hidden dimension of the LLM.
(Pre)-training Objective: Trained via Feature Alignment objectives where the projector receives all gradient updates while larger vision and language models are frozen, learning to translate between modalities.
Rationale: Vision and language models are trained in completely different latent spaces. Efficiently bridges this gap to allow the LLM to process visual tokens exactly as if they were foreign language text tokens.

Name: LoRA
Description: Widely adopted parameter-efficient fine-tuning method drastically reducing the number of trainable parameters. Allows massive models to be fine-tuned on single consumer GPUs.
Key Technique: Hypothesizes weight updates during fine-tuning have a low intrinsic rank. Freezes the massive weight matrix and adds a trainable low-rank decomposition matrix in parallel instead of updating full weights.
Architecture: Adds a parallel path calculating (B x A) alongside the frozen weight matrix (W). Trained low-rank matrices are multiplied and added back into W for inference, resulting in zero latency overhead.
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Fine-tuning a 70B parameter model requires vast VRAM just to store optimizer states. Reduces trainable parameters by up to 10,000x, democratizing fine-tuning while matching full fine-tuning accuracy.

Name: ReAct
Description: Foundational agentic prompting framework enabling LLMs to interface dynamically with external tools. Transforms an LLM from a static text generator into an active agent capable of iterative search, compute, and reasoning.
Key Technique: Interleaves internal reasoning steps (Thought) with external tool calls (Action) and environmental results (Observation). Creates a dynamic loop where the model plans moves based on real-time feedback.
Architecture: N/A
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: LLMs naturally hallucinate when lacking information. Forces the model to pause, actively retrieve missing information from an API or database, and incorporate real-world observations before concluding.

Name: ViDoRAG
Relationship: extends retrieval; visual retrieval; answer generation backbone; agentic reasoning pattern; sparse retrieval
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Multi-agent iterative reasoning (Seeker, Inspector, Answer agents); GMM-based dynamic hybrid retrieval (Text + Vision)
Architecture: Multi-agent RAG framework + GMM-based hybrid retriever
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Inference-only; LLMs like Qwen2.5-VL used as agents in prompt-driven generation

Name: Doc-React
Relationship: extends iteratively; dense retrieval encoder; sparse fallback; seq2seq reader
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Adaptive iterative retrieval framework that maximises entropy-normalised nutual information gain at each step
Architecture: Pipeline that wrap an existing MLLM (GPT-4o) and multimodal retrieval model (ColPali / VisRAG-Ret), MLLM acts as both judge and generator within ReAct-style loop
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: 1. Generator: produce answers from retrieved multimodal page substeps 2. Judge: evaluate information gaps and generates refined sub-queries via information differentiation

Name: Doc-Agent
Relationship: extends; agent loop pattern; reasoning traces; LLM backbone
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Multi-agent framework mimicking human reading, extracts a hierarchical XML tree-structured outline from the document, then use an actor agent with 5 retrieval tools to gather evidence, reviewer agent cross-checks answers from complementary sources/modalities, reflection-based memory bank for knowledge transfer across tasks
Architecture: Training-free pipeline: 1. Outline construction (adobe PDF extract --> XML tree) 2. Actor agent (LLM + 5 tools: search, get_section_content, get_image, get_page_images, get_table_image) 3. ReviewAgent (same toolset, independent trajectory) 4. Reflection Memory Module (LLM-generated verbal guidelines, cumulative string memory)
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Reasoning engine for all 3 agents: plans tool calls, reads retrieved multimodal content, generates answers, cross-checks answers, synthesises verbal memory guidelines

Name: MDocAgent
Relationship: text retrieval; visual retrieval; retrieve-then-read paradigm; agent orchestration; VLM backbone
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Design a multi-agent framework to integrate specialized agents from text/image understanding, critical information extraction and fianl answer prediction.
Architecture: Multi-agents structure combining several LLMs/MLLMs.
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: LLMs/MLLMs are frozen and adopted as specialised agents. Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct is used as text agent and Qwen2VL-7B-Instruct used for others. ColBERTv2 and ColPali used as vision and language retriever.

Name: M3DocRAG
Relationship: visual page retrieval; answer generation backbone; retrieval-augmented framework
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Retrieves relevant document pages using ColPali visual embeddings rather than OCR, preserving visual information --> MLLM (Qwen2-VL)
Architecture: PDF --> RGB images --> ColPali --> Retrieval --> MaxSim scoring --> MLLM Answer generation
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A (ColPali + Qwen2VL pretrained)
Rationale: Qwen2-VL 7B for answer generation

Name: VDocRAG
Relationship: contrastive visual embedding; VLM backbone; retrieve-then-read paradigm; parameter-efficient fine-tuning
Description: N/A
Key Technique: 2 component RAG framework (VDocRetriever + VDocGenerator) , retriever uses pretrained LVLM to compress entire page images into dense EOS token embeddings aligned with OCR content
Architecture: LVLM based dual encoding of queries and document images, maximum inner product search for retrieval, top-K retrieved page images concatenated with question is used to generate answer autoregressively
(Pre)-training Objective: VDocRetrieve pre-trained on 500K document image-OCR pairs using 2 objectives: RCR + RCG, LLM frozen, only LoRA + projector updated
Rationale: Phi3V (4.2B, primary) and Idefics3 (8B, alternative) for LLM backbone in VDocRetriever and as VDocGenerator for answer generation respectively

Name: MoLoRAG
Relationship: page visual encoder; VLM traversal backbone; retrieve-then-read framework
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Construct a page level graph, then uses a lightweight VLM as retrieval engine to traverse graph and score pages on semantic similarity and logical relevance to question
Architecture: ColPali to encode document and build page graph, Qwen2.5VL-3B as retrieval engine for logical relevance scoring, top K retrieved pages images fed into arbitrary LVLM for answer generation
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Qwen2.5-VL-3B for lightweight retrieval and assigning logical relevance scores, Qwen2.5-VL-7B for answer generator

Name: AVIR
Relationship: page relevance scoring backbone; adaptive answer generation backbone
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Lightweight Pix2Struct scores pages based on question relevance, then use K-means clustering (for long docs) or threshold filtering (for short docs) to select most relevant pages
Architecture: Frozen Pix2Struct encoder (100M) with 2 self attention layers and a sigmoid head for relevance scoring + frozen AWQ quantized Qwen2.5-VL-3B for answer generation, receiving only adaptively selected pages
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Qwen2.5-VL-3B-AWQ frozen answer generator

Name: KGP
Relationship: retrieval-augmented framework; text encoder for KG nodes; sparse retrieval
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Construct KG from documents (passages/pages/tables) + LLM-based graph traversal agent
Architecture: KG + LLM traversal (T5, LLaMA, ChatGPT) for retrieval + final answer from ChatGPT
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: T5 (or LLaMA): instruction-finetuned for graph traversal agent; LLM predicts next passage to retrieve

Name: LayTokenLLM
Relationship: architectural basis; LLM backbone; parameter-efficient fine-tuning; layout encoding inspiration
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Layout Token with New Positional Encoding Scheme for Layout Tokens without additional positional IDs.New Pretraining prediction pretraining.
Architecture: Layout Token is proposed to integrated into LLM as interleaved input with text tokens.
(Pre)-training Objective: Next Interleaved Text and Layout Token Prediction
Rationale: Contiuning Pretraining: LLama3-8B,Qwen1.5-7B as backbone for Text and Layout encoding.

Name: CREAM
Relationship: coarse-to-fine retrieval framework; text embedding encoder; seq2seq reader; efficient fine-tuning
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Coarse-to-fine retrieval: text embedding similarity (coarse phase) + LLM-based multi-round gruoping and reranking (find phase) to identify more relevant chunks
Architecture: Pix2Struct vision encoder extended via an attention pooling mechanism to handle multiple document pages --> projected into LLaMA2 7B via AdapterMLP layer
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Generates final answer conditioned on question + text chunks + visual document representations

Name: MHier-RAG
Relationship: hierarchical retrieval framework; visual page retrieval; answer generation backbone; sparse first-stage retrieval
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Multi-modal RAG with two complementary hierarchical index structures: (1) a Flattened In-Page Index enabling parent-page retrieval with LLM-based re-ranking to bridge modality disconnection within a page; (2) a Topological Cross-Page Index using Gaussian Mixture Model clustering and LLM-generated summaries for document-level long-distance reasoning across pages
Architecture: Training free: Docling PDF parser --> textual chunks (plain text, tables, layout) --> LVLM generated visual descriptions + raw images --> dual hierarchical index --> multi-granularity retriever --> LLM answer with CoT + structured output prompting
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: LVLM (Qwen-VL-Plus) converts visual content to text content descriptions, GPT-3-turbo summarises clustered cross-page chunks, Qwen-turb LVLM re-ranks retrieved parent pages, Qwen-turbo/GPT-4o/DeepSeek-chat/ERNIE-turbo LLM for final answer generation wtih CoT

Name: Self-Attention Scoring
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Convert document to image fed into Pix2Struct; page scoring to retrieve relevant pages using self-attention
Architecture: Self-attention top-K retrieval + Pix2Struct VLM
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: N/A

Name: InstructDr
Relationship: document encoder backbone; LLM decoder; parameter-efficient fine-tuning
Description: N/A
Key Technique: A Document-former is used to bridge multimodal document represenstation and LLM textual representation. Multi-task instruction tuning is applied.
Architecture: Cross-attention based Document Former is applied to bridge gap between vision encoder and LLM. Only Document-former and FFN layer are trainable.
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Frozen (FlanT5)

Name: PDF-WuKong
Relationship: VLM architecture template; language backbone; visual encoder; efficient fine-tuning
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Introduce a Sparse Sampling module (task) to identify and extract the most relevant text chunks or diagrams based similary. Contrastive learning
Architecture: The output from the text and image encoder are used to contrastive leraning and then feed into llm.
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: LLM (BGE-M3)

Name: Texthawk2
Relationship: visual encoder; LLM backbone option; architecture comparison; VLM template; visual encoder alternative
Description: N/A
Key Technique: 16x Visual Token Compression (ReSampling & Rearrangement), Unified Visual Encoder, Detection Head, Multi-level Cross Attention
Architecture: ViT + Resampler + LLM + Detection Head
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Qwen2-7B-Instruct Pre-training: Initially frozen during warm-up; then trained with LoRA, Fine tuning stage is trainable

Name: Leopard
Relationship: visual encoder; LLM backbone; architecture template; vision-language connector
Description: N/A
Key Technique: High quality instruction-tuning data. High-resolution multi-image encoding module.
Architecture: LEOPARD is decoder only vision language model, uses SigLIP-SO-400M as encoder, 2 layer MLP vison language connector and backbond LLM (LLaMA-3.1 or Mistral-7B)
(Pre)-training Objective: N/A
Rationale: Continue instruct-tuning: LLaVA, Idefics2 as backbone (MLLM )

Name: mPLUG-DocOwl2
Relationship: visual encoder; LLM backbone; VLM template; efficient fine-tuning
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Introduce a novel compressing architecture to greatly reduce visual tokens of high resolution document images to enable multi-page document understanding. It leverages a Shape-adaptive Cropping Module and a low resolution vision encoder to encode high-resolution document images. The glolab visual feature can be enhanced by applying cross-attention with layout aligned high-resolution visual features. For multipage scenarios, a special token is added before each document image.
Architecture: Build on Top of 1.5 version with a cross-attention absed High-reesolution DocCompressor.
(Pre)-training Objective: Single-image Pretraining, Multi-image Continue Pretraining
Rationale: LLM (mPLUG-Owl2) is frozeon execpt fine-tuning stage.

Name: LayoutLLM
Relationship: document encoder; LLM backbone; vision-language connector
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Layout instruction tuning combining layout-aware pre-training with novel LayoutCoT module in SFT
Architecture: LayoutLMv3-large as document encoder --> 2 MLP projectors (visual + text & layout) --> LLM backbone (causal decoder generates answer autoregressively)
(Pre)-training Objective: Layout aware pre-training across 3 levels: document level, region-level, segment level, LLM frozen during pre-taining, only projectors and DocPTM encoder updated
Rationale: Generate final answers (and LayoutCoT intermediate steps)

Name: Docopilot
Relationship: architecture template; long-context VLM backbone; efficient fine-tuning; visual encoder
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Native long-context MLLM with Multimodal Data Packing and Ring Attention to achieve VQA without reliance on RAG
Architecture: Standard ViT-MLP-LLM architecture using pre-trained InternViT300M, 2 layer MLP projector, pretrained InternLM2 LLM decoder
(Pre)-training Objective: InternVL2 (standard ViT + LLM)
Rationale: InternLM2 decoder-only LLM for generation

Name: Hi-VT5
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Visual, textual and layout features passed to T5 encoder to produce answer
Architecture: Hierarchical Transformer + per-page summary
(Pre)-training Objective: T5 pretrained on single image docs
Rationale: N/A

Name: GRAM
Relationship: cross-page text encoding; seq2seq backbone; visual encoder
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Extends pretrained SP Encoder + Decoder using Global-Local encoder blocks with document tokens that propagate information across pages
Architecture: Interleaved encoder-decoder transformer built on DocFormerv2, self attention applied only over cross-page doc tokens.
(Pre)-training Objective: DocFormerv2 (no further pre-training)
Rationale: N/A

Name: Arctic-TILT
Relationship: text encoder basis; encoder-decoder framework; visual feature extraction
Description: N/A
Key Technique: "Tensor Product" modality fusion mechanism integrated within each transformer block, chunked processing and sparse attention to handle long documents
Architecture: Encoder-decoder transformer based on T5, combined with contextualized vision from a U-Net with textual semantics using a fusion operation placed after the FFNs in every encoder layer
(Pre)-training Objective: Self supervised pretraining using pretrained T5, then further 900K steps self supervised pretraining on document datasets after introducing TILT architecture modifications
Rationale: End-to-end multimodal processor directly consuming T, V, L inputs to generate text based answers

Name: DocLLM
Relationship: disentangled spatial attention basis; LLM backbone
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Extends standard LLMs with a disentangled spatial attention mechanism to capture cross-modal dependencies without vision encoder, pre-training uses autoregressive block infilling objective rather than next-token prediction
Architecture: Causal decoder-only transformer (Falcon-1B or Llama2-7B) extended withspatia hidden vectors S and standard text hidden vectors H, attention decomposed into 4 disentangled scores (T2T, T2S, S2T, S2S) with only S2S activated at inference
(Pre)-training Objective: Self supervised autoregressive block infilling: randomly mask text blocks, shuffles them, and reconstructs them sequentially using both preceding and following context
Rationale: Single model performing all tasks: generate answers, extracts key values, classifies documents, fact verification

Name: DocSLM
Relationship: text encoder; seq2seq compression backbone; visual encoder; parameter-efficient fine-tuning
Description: N/A
Key Technique: hierarchical multimodal compressor fuses visual, OCR text and layout features into fixed 576 token page representation + Streaming Abstention mechanism that processes document segments sequentially with entropy-based uncertainty calibration for constant memory footprint
Architecture: SigLIP2 vision encoder + PaddleOCR extractor --> Local OCR compression --> Global visual compression --> MLP adapter --> Qwen2.5-1.5B SLM
(Pre)-training Objective: 1. OCR alignment 2. image compression 3. document compression (multi-page)
Rationale: Generator: receives 576-token pae representations and natural language query, produces segment-level answer predictions with token-level entropy uncertainty scores

Name: DocLayLLM
Dependencies: LayoutLMv3; LLaMA 3; LoRA; MLP Projector; DiT
Relationship: document encoder; LLM backbone; parameter-efficient fine-tuning; vision-language connector; document visual backbone
Description: N/A
Key Technique: Efficient OCR dependent MLLM extension of LLMs that integrates document patch tokens (visual) and 2D positional tokens (layout) directly into the LLM's inpt squence alongside OCR text, combined with CoT pretraining and CoT annealing
Architecture: OCR engine --> text tokens + image patch tokens + bounding box tokens --> unified LLM (Llama2-7B / Llama3-8B) with LoRA; no separate document encoder
(Pre)-training Objective: (not strictly pretraining) broad instruction tuning across many document tasks to align visual and layout tokens with the LLM, using ~3.1M diverse pairs
Rationale: unified encoder and generator (Llama2 or 3) directly encodes all 3 modalities in its input sequence and generates the answer
